Article Title: Nominee vs legal heir: What is the difference and who has claim on assets? All you need to know
Article Text:
AI image

Who is a Nominee?



Who is a Legal Heir?

Difference between a Nominee and a Legal heir

Aspect Nominee Legal Heir Role Custodian Owner Transfer of Assets Can be done without any legal formalities. Requires testamentary documents for proof of ownership. Powers No power to transact, use, or transfer assets. Power to use and transact assets as an owner. Appointment Owner can appoint anyone, but some institutions specify certain conditions. Determined by succession/personal laws. Legal Right Cannot contest ownership legally. Can contest ownership in a court of law.

When individuals open bank accounts, invest in mutual funds, or buy insurance, they are often asked to name a nominee.However, many people mistakenly assume that the nominee automatically inherits the assets. In reality, the nominee is not always the final recipient — the ownership rights belong to the legal heir, reported ET.Understanding the distinction between these two is essential to ensure that your wealth is passed on to the intended beneficiaries.A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets.The nominee’s role is limited to holding and managing the assets until they are legally transferred to the rightful heirs.According to ET, the nominee cannot sell, trade, or make any transactional decisions about the assets.The account or policy owner can appoint anyone — family, friends, or acquaintances — as a nominee, though financial institutions often prefer close family members.In some cases, the nominee and the legal heir can be the same person, which helps in ensuring a smoother transfer of assets.The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away.To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will or other testamentary proof.If there is no will — a situation known as dying intestate — the heir is determined as per the country’s succession or personal laws, reported ET.While a nominee can initially access the assets, only the legal heir can claim them permanently or challenge ownership in court.Experts quoted by ET emphasise that having clarity on this difference helps avoid disputes and ensures that the savings reach the intended family members or beneficiaries.Therefore, it is advisable to regularly update both nominees and wills to align with one’s estate planning goals.
Article Summary: A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets. The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away. To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will.

Article Title: Life Certificate 2025: Step-by-step guide to generate and submit Jeevan Pramaan online and offline
Article Text:
What is a Life Certificate and why does it matter



Annual submission deadline for pensioners in 2025



1 November and 30 November.

How to generate Jeevan Pramaan online: Step-by-step process



Step 1: Biometric device registration

Step 2: Authentication and certificate generation

Electronic delivery and access of Digital Life Certificate



How can you submit a Life Certificate in 2025



Offline submission at a bank branch or CSC centre

Online submission via Jeevan Pramaan Portal

Submission through the UMANG App

Who needs to submit the Life Certificate



Central and State Government pensioners

Defence pensioners

Family pensioners in the event of the pensioner’s death

Pensioners from public sector undertakings

Key details required before submitting the Life Certificate



Aadhaar Number

Registered Mobile Number linked with Aadhaar

Pension Payment Order (PPO) Number

Bank Account Number where the pension is credited

Name of the Pension Sanctioning Authority

Is Aadhaar mandatory for a digital Life Certificate



Legal validity of Jeevan Pramaan



How Jeevan Pramaan simplifies pensioner verification



Every year, lakhs of pensioners across India must prove that they are still alive to continue receiving their pension. This verification, known as the Life Certificate or Digital Life Certificate (DLC), is a crucial and mandatory step in ensuring that pension payments are made only to eligible recipients, preventing fraud and misuse.Traditionally, this process involved visiting the bank or pension office in person, standing in long queues, and submitting a signed paper certificate. However, with the government’s focus on digitisation and technological advancement, this process has become much simpler, faster, and more convenient. Pensioners can now submit their certificates digitally from the comfort of their homes using Jeevan Pramaan, a secure, user-friendly, and Aadhaar-based platform.A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries.The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric-enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.Introduced by the Government of India, it uses Aadhaar-based biometric authentication using either fingerprint or iris scan to verify a pensioner’s identity. Once verified, a digital certificate is automatically generated and shared with authorised pension disbursing agencies.This online process eliminates the need for physical verification, providing elderly pensioners with a simpler and more accessible way to maintain their pension benefits.According to PIB, The submission of the Life Certificate is mandatory for all pensioners who wish to continue receiving their pension without interruption. For the year 2025, the standard submission period is betweenTo make the process more comfortable for the elderly, the government allows pensioners aged 80 years and above to begin the submission process earlier, from 1 October 2025. This extended window gives senior citizens more time and flexibility to complete the procedure without facing last-minute difficulties.The Jeevan Pramaan process involves two main steps: registering the biometric device and generating the digital certificate.Before using the service, pensioners or operators must register their biometric device such as a fingerprint or iris scanner. During registration, the user provides their Aadhaar number and mobile number, after which an OTP (One-Time Password) is sent to their mobile. After entering the OTP, they must provide their name and email ID before scanning their fingerprint or iris.Once authentication is complete, the system confirms that device registration was successful, allowing the user to move to the next step.In this step, the pensioner enters personal details such as Aadhaar number, mobile number, name, PPO number, pension type, sanctioning authority, disbursing agency, and bank account details. The system also asks whether the pensioner has remarried or is re-employed.After entering all details, the pensioner scans their fingerprint or iris using the registered biometric device. Upon successful authentication, the Digital Life Certificate is generated, and an SMS acknowledgment with the Jeevan Pramaan Certificate ID is sent to the registered mobile number.This certificate is automatically stored in the Life Certificate Repository, accessible anytime to both pensioners and pension agencies.Once the Digital Life Certificate is created, it can be electronically delivered to the concerned Pension Disbursing Agency (PDA). PDAs can log in to the Jeevan Pramaan portal to view, verify, or download the certificate directly.Pensioners can also download a PDF copy of their certificate by visiting the Jeevan Pramaan website and entering their Pramaan ID or Aadhaar number. This ensures transparency and easy access for future reference.The government provides several options to submit the Life Certificate, allowing pensioners to choose the method that suits them best.Pensioners who prefer traditional methods can visit their bank branch or a Citizen Service Centre (CSC) to submit their Life Certificate physically. Trained staff at these centres assist pensioners in verifying their details and completing the process.For those unable to travel due to age or health issues, India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) offers a doorstep banking service. Pensioners can schedule an appointment, and an IPPB representative will visit their home to complete the submission using biometric authentication. This service is available for both IPPB and non-IPPB account holders.For pensioners comfortable with technology, the Jeevan Pramaan portal provides a completely online process. By visiting https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in, pensioners can submit their Digital Life Certificate using a computer, laptop, or smartphone equipped with a fingerprint or iris scanner.The system uses the Aadhaar platform to authenticate biometric details. Once verification is successful, a Digital Life Certificate is generated and stored in a secure online repository. Pension disbursing agencies can then directly access the certificate, removing the need for physical paperwork.The UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app offers another easy option. Pensioners can download the app from the Google Play Store, search for “Jeevan Pramaan,” and select “Generate Life Certificate.”After entering the necessary details such as Aadhaar number, PPO number, and type of pension, pensioners must authenticate themselves using biometric verification. The app then generates a Digital Life Certificate (DLC), which is automatically shared with the pension disbursing authority.All pensioners who receive government, defence, or private sector pensions through authorised disbursing agencies are required to submit their Life Certificate every year. This includes:Failure to submit the certificate within the stipulated period may result in temporary suspension of pension payments until verification is completed.To ensure a smooth submission process, pensioners should keep certain details and documents ready. These include:Having these details ready helps to avoid delays or errors during authentication, whether submitting online or offline.Yes, an Aadhaar number or Virtual ID (VID) is mandatory to generate a Digital Life Certificate. The entire Jeevan Pramaan system is based on Aadhaar authentication, which ensures accuracy and prevents fraud. Pensioners must make sure their Aadhaar is linked with their mobile number and bank account before attempting to generate the certificate.The Digital Life Certificate is fully valid and legally recognised under the Information Technology Act. Pension disbursing authorities across India accept it as an official document, equivalent to the traditional paper-based life certificate.Its biometric verification system makes it secure and tamper-proof, reducing the chances of wrongful pension disbursal or misuse of pension funds.Before the introduction of Jeevan Pramaan, pensioners often faced difficulties in submitting their certificates, especially those with health problems or limited mobility. The digital system has removed these challenges, allowing pensioners to submit their verification anytime and anywhere.By integrating biometric technology and Aadhaar authentication, Jeevan Pramaan has streamlined pension management, improving efficiency for both pensioners and government departments. It also enhances transparency and ensures that genuine beneficiaries receive payments on time.
Article Summary: A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries. The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric- enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.

Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: IRCTC's new website goes live: How it makes ticket booking easier for consumers
Article Text:
What changes for passengers?

Cleaner interface: The new website removes unnecessary captchas, pop-ups, flashing graphics and other distracting elements for a simpler booking experience.

The new website removes unnecessary captchas, pop-ups, flashing graphics and other distracting elements for a simpler booking experience. Seat availability in one view: Travellers can now check seat availability across all classes from a single screen instead of viewing each class separately.

Travellers can now check seat availability across all classes from a single screen instead of viewing each class separately. Faster checkout: The booking process has been shortened by reducing the number of steps needed to complete a ticket purchase.

The booking process has been shortened by reducing the number of steps needed to complete a ticket purchase. Quicker repeat bookings: Passenger details will be saved, making future bookings faster and more convenient.



IRCTC's new beta website: Fewer clicks, faster process — your next ticket booking on IRCTC just got easier! The ministry of railways has unveiled a beta version of its revamped website, featuring a cleaner interface, faster checkout and saved passenger details for repeat bookings.The beta version of the Indian railway catering and tourism corporation (IRCTC) website was launched at 2100 hrs on Wednesday. A link to the new portal has also been added to the homepage of the existing IRCTC website.The ministry has asked users to try the platform and share feedback before the final version is rolled out.The redesign was shaped with inputs from students of the Malaviya National Institute of Technology (MNIT), Jaipur, who suggested improvements to the website's look and feel during an interaction with railway minister Ashwini Vaishnaw. The students were made part of the design process, and their feedback was used to prepare the portal for public trial.The ministry said the beta release is intended to collect user feedback, which will be used to make further improvements before the website is fully rolled out.Passengers can share suggestions on the simplified features, and these may be incorporated into future releases to improve customer satisfaction.The IRCTC website, first launched in 2002, currently handles an average of around 14.5 lakh ticket bookings every day. According to the ministry, the website has received several complaints over the years regarding its functioning and features, prompting the redesign.The revamped portal will also be integrated with the new Passenger Reservation Engine, which is being upgraded in parallel. The reservation system, which powers various train booking applications, has undergone extensive revamp work while remaining operational throughout the upgrade process.The ministry said the new Passenger Reservation Engine will be launched very soon, and the fully functional new IRCTC portal integrated with it is expected to be available in a few weeks.Users have been requested to explore the beta website and provide suggestions that can help further improve the platform before its full launch.
Article Summary: The beta version of the Indian railway catering and tourism corporation (IRCTC) website was launched at 2100 hrs on Wednesday. Travellers can now check seat availability across all classes from a single screen instead of viewing each class separately. The booking process has been shortened by reducing the number of steps needed to complete a ticket purchase.

Why Turkey remains a global leader in hair transplantation

Disclaimer - The above content is non-editorial, and TIL hereby disclaims any and all warranties, expressed or implied, relating to it, and does not guarantee, vouch for or necessarily endorse any of the content.

Over the past decade, Turkey has quietly become the world's busiest destination for hair restoration. The reasons are not mysterious: a dense concentration of specialised surgeons, a healthcare infrastructure built around international patients, and treatment packages that often cost a fraction of what the same procedure runs in Western Europe or North America. Add a city like Istanbul, where you can recover between two continents, with the Bosphorus on one side and centuries of history on the other, and it becomes clear why so many people turn a medical appointment into a genuine trip.But choosing a clinic from abroad is harder than choosing a hotel. You are trusting a team you have never met with a result you will carry for the rest of your life. This guide ranks ten of the most established hair transplant clinics in Istanbul and pairs the list with practical travel advice, so you can plan both the procedure and the days around it with confidence.The industry's premier medical R&D hub and pioneer of ultra-dense hair restoration.A multidisciplinary board of surgeons and biomedical researchers with over 15 years of combined clinical experience. Vera Clinic is not just a hair transplant centre; it is a recognised research and development institution that actively develops the industry's next-generation technologies. The team is behind innovations, including theCVD lab-grown diamond-tipped instrument for precision micro-incisions andfor maximum density. Named Best Hair Transplant Clinic in Turkey by the European Award in Medicine (2021) , and home to, which trains physicians and runs ongoing clinical research, the clinic gives patients access to patented, next-generation methods inside a five-star facility.Expert-led aesthetic restoration overseen by a Cleveland Clinic-trained plastic surgeon.Directed by Dr Ekrem Keskin, with extensive verified acclaim across major international medical review platforms.Capital Hair Center places a board-certified plastic surgeon at the helm of every procedure, bringing genuine precision to hairline design and facial harmony. Sapphire FUE and DHI are complemented by proprietary treatments like OzonFect and needle-free anaesthesia. A strong option for patients who want their transplant handled with the rigour of reconstructive surgery.A boutique clinic focused on personalised treatment and individual hairline design.Founded in 2016 by Şule Ölmez and supported by an experienced medical team.Unlike larger clinics that perform a high volume of procedures, Sule Hair Transplant emphasises a more personalised patient experience. The clinic offers Sapphire FUE and DHI techniques, with treatment plans tailored to each patient's goals and hair characteristics. Patients who value direct communication, detailed consultation, and a boutique environment often find the clinic's approach appealing. Its all-inclusive packages and structured aftercare programme also make the process straightforward for international visitors.A boutique approach pairing AI mapping technology with low daily patient volumes.A boutique team headed by Dr Merve Kaya.Este Favor focuses on lower daily intakes, so each case receives dedicated attention, pairing AI-assisted planning with the now-familiar combination of treatment plus travel coordination. It is a careful, detail-oriented option for medical tourists who want a smaller-scale experience.A long-standing, multidisciplinary institution with two decades of experience.Boasting a 4.8-star rating from thousands of verified reviews and global recognition in outlets.Aslı Tarcan Clinic draws on two decades of experience, covering Sapphire FUE, DHI, and Robotic DHI, with optional PRP and stem cell therapies. Multilingual staff, VIP transfers, and structured aftercare make it a solid choice for patients seeking a review-backed clinic with a long track record and all-inclusive setup.A straightforward, package-based experience for international medical tourists.An established Istanbul provider working in the international medical tourism space.Medart bundles the procedure with all the practical essentials, consultation, transfers, accommodation guidance, and aftercare that overseas patients look for. It's a strong pick for those comparing several mainstream Istanbul options and wanting a clear, predictable, all-in-one process.One of the largest hair transplant providers in Turkey, serving a high volume of international patients each year.Large multidisciplinary medical team and dedicated international patient services.Elithair has built its reputation around accessibility, structured treatment pathways, and experience managing patients from around the world. The clinic offers FUE-based hair restoration procedures supported by multilingual coordinators, comprehensive treatment packages, and established pre- and post-operative processes. With consultation locations in several countries and a large operational infrastructure, it is often considered by patients looking for a streamlined and highly organised treatment experience.Streamlined, end-to-end care built around the international patient.A coordinated team specialising in travel-and-treatment packages for overseas patients.AHD Clinic delivers a smooth, fully managed journey from initial consultation through aftercare. Its appeal lies in the seamless coordination that has become the Istanbul standard, ideal for patients who want a hassle-free, supported experience from start to finish.World-class Sapphire and DHI expertise on a high-volume scale.A high-volume international practice headed by Dr Levent Acar.Dr Acar is highly respected across Europe, and the clinic has built significant visibility in the international market. Custom-built for international patients, Cosmedica offers seamlessly structured all-inclusive packages using state-of-the-art techniques, making it a go-to for those comparing the larger, well-known practices.Full-service treatment paired with comprehensive logistical support.An Istanbul team focused primarily on serving overseas patients.Transmed combines the surgical procedure with airport transfers, accommodation guidance, and structured follow-up, simplifying the entire experience of travelling abroad for treatment. It suits patients who want both quality care and worry-free logistics in one package.Turkey's position as the world's leading hair transplant destination is not the result of marketing alone. It is built on decades of experience, high patient volumes, specialised medical teams, and continuous investment in hair restoration technologies.For international patients comparing options, the search for the best hair transplant clinic in Turkey often extends beyond price alone and includes factors such as surgeon involvement, patient support, and long-term results.As competition has increased, leading clinics have focused on improving patient outcomes, refining techniques, and delivering more natural-looking results. For patients willing to research their options carefully, Turkey continues to offer a combination of expertise, accessibility, and value that remains difficult to match elsewhere.
Article Summary: Turkey has quietly become the world's busiest destination for hair restoration. A dense concentration of specialised surgeons, a healthcare infrastructure built around international patients, and treatment packages that often cost a fraction of what the same procedure runs in Western Europe or North America. But choosing a clinic from abroad is harder than choosing a hotel.

Article Title: OnePlus confirms exit from North America, Europe: What the company said
Article Text:
image credit : One plus

OxygenOS is officially ‘dead’



Here is OnePlus’ official statement on OS change

"As part of an operational adjustment to our software strategy, OnePlus devices will update from OxygenOS to ColorOS. This software update enables us to streamline software development, accelerate update cycles to our community, improve software quality, and make better use of our shared engineering and R&D capabilities to deliver a smarter, smoother software experience.

During the course of this software update, our commitment to fast, intuitive and user-centric experiences for OnePlus users remains unchanged. We will share more details on the rollout roadmap at a later stage."
Article Summary: OxygenOS is officially ‘dead’ and will be replaced by ColorOS. OnePlus says it will streamline software development, accelerate update cycles and improve software quality. The company will share more details on the rollout roadmap at a later stage.

Article Title: Samsung unveils the Music Studio Series in India: Price, features and more
Article Text:
Samsung Music Studio Series price in India



Samsung Music Studio Series features



Samsung has introduced the Music Studio Series, a new lineup of Wi-Fi speakers that combine aesthetics with immersive sound. These include the Music Studio 5 (LS50H) and Music Studio 7 (LS70H). The Music Studio Series is the latest addition to Samsung’s expanding audio ecosystem, offering integration with Samsung TVs and soundbars. Designed by designer Erwan Bouroullec, these speakers feature an iconic dot design inspired by universal symbols in music and art, blending elegance with functionality.Samsung’s Music Studio series is now available at Samsung.com, leading offline retailers, and major e-commerce platforms, starting at a price of Rs 27,900.“Samsung has been transforming home audio in India with advanced audio technology and smart features. We’re continuing that legacy with next-generation sound devices designed to deliver a rich, expressive performance for any space and moment. We’re proud to continue this journey with new Music Studio series that bring rich, immersive audio to every Indian home,” said Viplesh Dang, Vice-President of the Visual Display Business at Samsung India.Samsung has introduced two new Wi-Fi speakers, the Music Studio 5 and Music Studio 7, as part of its Music Studio Series. These speakers are designed to enhance the integrated ecosystem by combining aesthetics with immersive sound technology. Samsung says that these new models enable a wider range of sound system combinations, maximising the audio-visual experience. Additionally, they offer richer sound experiences while also enhancing aesthetic harmony with users’ spaces, allowing users to enjoy an improved audio environment.Music Studio 7 (LS70H model) delivers 3.1.1-channel spatial audio through left, front, right and top-firing speakers for natural 3D immersion. Samsung’s Audio Lab Pattern Control Technology reduces signal overlap for cleaner directionality, while AI Dynamic Bass Control delivers deep bass with minimal distortion. It functions as a standalone immersive speaker or can be paired with additional Samsung sound devices or Samsung TVs as well.Music Studio 5 (LS50H model) is tuned by Samsung Audio Lab, and uses a woofer and dual tweeters with a built-in waveguide for clear, balanced sound, while AI Dynamic Bass Control deepens low frequencies without distortion. Wi-Fi casting, streaming services, voice control and Bluetooth via Samsung Seamless Codec provide smart, seamless connectivity.The Music Studio Series integrates with Samsung’s SmartThings app, allowing users to control sound settings, manage group playback, and access music streaming services. With Q-Symphony, users can pair up to five sound devices with a Samsung TV.
Article Summary: Samsung has introduced two new Wi-Fi speakers, the Music Studio 5 and Music Studio 7, as part of its Music Studio Series. These speakers are designed to enhance the integrated ecosystem by combining aesthetics with immersive sound technology. The Music Studio series is now available at Samsung.com, leading offline retailers, and major e-commerce platforms, starting at a price of Rs 27,900.

Article Title: Boat partners Raftaar and Krsna's Legacy platform to support India's next generation of hip-hop artists
Article Text:
Legacy platform focuses on emerging hip-hop talent



"At boAt, we've always believed that the strongest communities create the biggest cultural movements. Every generation has creators who redefine music, challenge convention, and inspire millions.

Legacy represents that spirit. Through this partnership, we're proud to support the next generation of artists and the communities helping them build India's cultural future."

"Having been associated with boAt as a brand ambassador, it’s exciting to now extend that partnership through Legacy. It’s great to see a brand continue to invest in the culture beyond campaigns and become part of a platform that helps discover and nurture the next generation of hip-hop talent.

Hip-hop has always been driven by the community, and every new voice deserves the opportunity to be heard."

"Every generation creates its own sound and its own culture. Legacy is about discovering those voices, but it’s equally about the community that supports them. We’re proud to have a brand like boAt backing this movement. When brands invest in communities rather than simply moments, they help strengthen the entire ecosystem and empower the next generation of artists."

Domestic audio brand Boat has announced a partnership with Legacy. This is a community-led hip-hop talent platform judged by rappers Raftaar and KR$NA. The collaboration aims to support emerging artists and creator communities as the audio and wearables brand expands its focus on music and youth culture in India.The company said the partnership is part of its ongoing efforts to back creators, communities and cultural movements. Through Legacy, boAt plans to support aspiring hip-hop artists and provide visibility to new talent emerging from India's growing rap scene.Legacy is a hip-hop reality platform designed to identify and develop new rap artists. Led by Raftaar and KR$NA, the show follows participants through multiple stages, including cyphers, battles and collaborations, before selecting a winner.Legacy follows a three-stage format. The competition begins with the Top 30 and then progresses to live head-to-head battles with the Top 15 before concluding with Sudden Death rounds and the Grand Finale with the Top 10.Commenting on the partnership, a Boat spokesperson said,KR$NA said,Raftaar added,The company said the collaboration reflects its broader strategy of working with creators, musicians and community-led platforms as new forms of music and creator-driven entertainment continue to evolve in India.
Article Summary: Legacy is a hip-hop reality platform designed to identify and develop new rap artists. Led by Raftaar and KR$NA, the show follows participants through multiple stages, including cyphers, battles and collaborations. The company said the partnership reflects its broader strategy of working with creators, musicians and community-led platforms.

Article Title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide: Expected specifications, price in India and launch date
Article Text:
Representational image

Expected specs of Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide



Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide launch date



Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide expected price in India and USA



Samsung Galaxy Unpacked will be on July 22. At this year’s event, the South Korean giant is expected to launch three foldable smartphones: Galaxy Z Fold 8, Galaxy Z Flip 8 and a brand-new entry: the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide. A report by tech outlet WinFuture has detailed key specifications, features and price point for the upcoming series, giving users something to look forward to. The leak outlines a clear two-tier strategy from Samsung, revealing how the “Wide” model stacks up against the premium variants.As per the report, both devices are expected to be powered by Qualcomm's flagship Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy processor, which is the same custom chip found in the Galaxy S26 series.The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide is reported to be designed for users who want an expansive tablet-like experience without paying the extreme Ultra premium. The rumoured ‘Wide’ model is expected to feature a 5.5-inch outer cover screen with a 1972 x 1248 resolution. When opened, it may reveal a massive 7.6-inch main inner display boasting a 2448 x 1848 resolution, slightly taller than a traditional 16:10 aspect ratio, and 120HZ refresh rate.The Wide may feature a dual-camera setup on the back, consisting of a 50MP main wide lens and a 50MP ultrawide sensor. For video calls and selfies, it is reported to sport 10MP front-facing cameras on both the inside and outside screens. The phone is likely to pack a 4,800 mAh battery and support for Samsung’s faster 45W wired charging speed.It is expected to ship with 12GB of RAM across three storage configurations: 256GB, 512GB, and a massive 1TB option. The phone may be available in four youthful colorways: Graphite, Cream, Lavender and Pistachio.While Samsung has not officially confirmed the release details, reports and rumours claim that the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide may be launched alongside the other Galaxy foldable phones on July 22.According to the multiple leaks, Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide may start at $1,899 for the base 256GB model. In Europe, it is expected to start at €1,999. In India, historical pricing trends suggest that due to local manufacturing and import structures, the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide may launch at a price around Rs 1,64,999 to Rs 1,74,999.
Article Summary: The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide is reported to be designed for users who want an expansive tablet-like experience without paying the extreme Ultra premium. The rumoured ‘Wide’ model is expected to feature a 5.5-inch outer cover screen with a 1972 x 1248 resolution. When opened, it may reveal a massive 7.6-inch main inner display boasting a 2448 x 1848 resolution, slightly taller than a traditional 16:10 aspect ratio.
